blocks.text: Jennifer Herrera,

Got everything your team sent over, and there is nothing outstanding on your side for the moment. Amy Carter is drafting the first cut of the core messages for the {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} leadership voice against those inputs, and I would rather she land it right than land it fast, so look for a version worth your time early next week.

One thing that would help now: point me to anyone besides you who needs to read language before it goes out. If we know that from the first draft we route it correctly instead of circling back, and if it is only you for the moment, tell me and we keep the loop tight.
John Macias
Jennifer Herrera,

Thank you, that settles who holds sign-off. I am partway through checking each claim in the draft against the source documents your finance team provided, and most of them reconcile cleanly. A couple of the numbers on the {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} revenue history do not yet tie back to a source I can cite, and I would rather confirm those with you than carry a figure I cannot stand behind.

Could you tell me whether those two lines should quote the audited numbers or the internally reported ones? Once I know which basis you want used, I can close out the fact check and pass a clean draft to John Macias for his read before anything reaches you.

Best,
Amy Carter
Jennifer Herrera,

Quick add to Amy Carter's note before you reply. While you settle which figures we quote, I want to get a working session on the calendar so we can pressure-test the full message set with you in the room rather than trade it over email.
A few things to make that hour count:

items: Send two or three windows late this month that suit your calendar; thirty minutes is plenty on our end.
Bring whoever owns the {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} board narrative, since that is the audience this language has to survive.
No prep needed beyond the two numbers Amy Carter flagged; we walk in with the draft.

blocks.text: John Macias
Jennifer Herrera,

That works, and thank you for turning it around so quickly. I will quote the audited numbers for the two revenue lines and note the basis in the margin, so there is no question later about where either figure came from.

Ahead of the session John Macias is setting up, I will circulate the current message draft and a short agenda a couple of days out, so you can read it cold before we sit down. If any of the {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} positioning strikes you wrong on that first pass, mark it up and we will start the session there.
Amy Carter
Meeting Minutes -- Executive Messaging for {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}}
Working session to review the draft message framework and agree what a client-ready version still needs. Minutes prepared by Amy Carter.
Attendees
Discussion

The session opened on the draft core-message set. John Macias walked Jennifer Herrera through the three leadership lines as drafted and the reasoning behind the order they sit in. Jennifer Herrera agreed with the first two without change and pushed on the third, which she felt leaned harder on scale than the board would find credible. The group agreed to recast that line around the operating record and let the scale figures support it rather than carry it.

Amy Carter reported that the fact check against the source documents is substantially complete. The two figures on the {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} revenue history that had been open were confirmed on the audited basis during the session, and Amy Carter will note that basis in the margin of the framework so the source is never in question. One proof point behind the second line still needs a confirming source; Jennifer Herrera undertook to pull it from her operations team.

The group also talked through the settings where the language will first be tested and who the real audience is in each. Jennifer Herrera noted that the {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} board is the audience the third line has to survive, and that anything reading as promotional would cost more credibility than it bought. Amy Carter captured that as a working test for the material: each claim carries its own proof point, and any line that cannot be backed comes out rather than gets softened. John Macias asked that the next draft set each proof point beside its line so the two are read together.

On approvals, Jennifer Herrera confirmed she holds final sign-off on any language that leaves the company and named no additional internal reviewer at this stage. The group agreed the sequence for the coming weeks: settle the framework, build the supporting material around it, and hold a second review before anything is used in a setting that carries weight. John Macias flagged that the value of the work is a steady voice that survives repetition, so the group agreed not to widen the message set but to deepen the material behind the three lines already agreed.

Before closing, the group set the working cadence for the next stretch. Amy Carter will keep one live version of the framework and the material in the shared engagement file, so there is a single source everyone works from and no draft goes missing in a personal inbox. John Macias will carry a short status to Jennifer Herrera on a regular rhythm, so the client hears of any change from the firm rather than around it. Jennifer Herrera asked that anything intended for a public setting reach her with enough lead time to read it cold, and the group agreed to build that reading window into the schedule rather than treat it as an afterthought. No item was left without an owner, and the list below records what each person is holding.
Action Items

rows: Recast the third leadership line around the operating record and note the audited basis for the two revenue figures in the margin
Confirm the outstanding proof point with the {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} operations team
Circulate the revised framework and schedule the second review session
Begin drafting the supporting material behind the three agreed lines

blocks.text: Status Report -- Executive Messaging for {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}}

Prepared by John Macias for the {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} leadership team. This report covers the work through the midpoint of the engagement and sets out what is done, what is next, and where the risks sit.
Summary

The engagement is on track and roughly at its midpoint. The core message framework is settled: three leadership lines, each carrying its own proof point, agreed with your team in the working session and now the fixed reference for everything we draft. Settling that framework was the gate the rest of the work waited on, and closing it has let us move from the harder question of what to say to the more mechanical one of building the material that carries it. Nothing on the critical path is behind. The one open data point flagged at the last review has been confirmed, the fact check against your source documents is complete, and the two revenue figures that were unresolved are now cited on the audited basis. In short, the foundation is in place, and the second half of the engagement is about production and rehearsal rather than decisions.
Accomplishments This Period
The period since kickoff has been about getting the framework right and the facts under it solid, on the principle that everything downstream leans on both. Concretely, this period we have:

items: Closed the message framework after the working session, recasting the third line around the operating record so it reads as credible to the {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} board rather than as a claim about size.

Completed the fact check on your source documents. Amy Carter confirmed the two revenue figures on the audited basis and noted that basis in the framework, so no claim now rests on a number we cannot cite.
Resolved the outstanding proof point behind the second line using the confirming source your operations team provided.
Begun the supporting material behind the three agreed lines, with each claim drafted beside its proof point so the two are always read together.
Set a working test for everything that follows: any line that cannot be backed comes out rather than gets softened.

blocks.text: Budget

The engagement is a fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-2023-001.fee}}, and the work to date sits well within that plan. We bill against the fixed fee rather than hours, so the cost does not move with the number of drafts or calls; the figure agreed at the outset is the figure you pay. Approved out-of-pocket costs, should any arise, are passed through at cost and listed line by line. Nothing in the work so far puts the budget under pressure, and we do not foresee anything in the back half that would.
Upcoming Work

The next stretch is production and rehearsal. Amy Carter will finish the supporting material so that every leadership line has a complete set of backing behind it, drafted to the same standard the framework set. Once the material is drafted we will hold a second review with your team before any of it is used in a setting that carries weight, so nothing goes out that you have not seen. From there the work shifts from the page to the people: the language only holds up if your leaders sound the same from one appearance to the next, so we will spend time preparing them to use it rather than just handing it over. In parallel we will map the first public settings where the messages will actually be tested, so that when the moment comes the words have already been rehearsed against it and nobody meets an audience cold. None of this reopens the framework; it builds on the version already agreed.
Risks

items: Review timing. The schedule assumes your team can turn the second review inside a normal window. A long gap there is the single thing most likely to compress the back half, so we would rather book that review now than chase it later.

Late changes to the message set. The value of this work is a voice that holds up under repetition, so reopening the three agreed lines late would cost more than it returns. We would treat any such change as a deliberate decision, not a quiet edit.
External timing. Where a message is tied to an announcement your side controls, we take our cue from you on timing; a date that moves on your end moves the rehearsal that depends on it.

blocks.text: I will keep this report on the same rhythm through the second half of the engagement. The pattern that has worked so far, settle the hard questions early and then build steadily against them, is the one we intend to hold to. Anything that needs a decision from your side, John Macias will bring to you directly and early rather than let it sit, because on this kind of work late notice is the expensive kind.
